---
title: "Functional_Factors_Analysis"
author: "Zhongzhi"
date: "2026-03-16"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, include=FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(echo = TRUE)
```


### PERMANOVA analysis for beta diversity against environmental factors
```{r}
### To be modified Note
#Remove Sample NW046
#Select one of the three NW016 Sample.
library(dplyr)
library(tibble)
library(readr)
library(vegan)
library(ggplot2)
library(ggrepel)
library(compositions)
library(ape)

# Read in Normalized Pathway Abundance
path_abun_table <- file.path("../../data/Metagenomics/HUMAnN_func/Normalized_Tables/", "merged_pathabundance_relab.tsv")
path_abun <- read.table(path_abun_table, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", comment.char = "")


### Remove "Unmapped" and "Unintegrated"
colnames(path_abun)[1]
colnames(path_abun)[1] <- "Pathway"
nrow(path_abun)
select_path_abun <- path_abun %>% filter(!Pathway %in% c("UNMAPPED", "UNINTEGRATED"))

### Remove pathways with taxonomic annotations
select_path_abun <- select_path_abun %>% filter (!grepl("\\|", Pathway))

# Total number of the pathways
nrow(select_path_abun)

# Remove not interested samples (without metadata and multiple sample for one individual)
ncol(select_path_abun)
select_path_abun <- select_path_abun %>% select(-NW046_5_all_merged_Abundance)
select_path_abun <- select_path_abun %>% select(-NW016_2_2_all_merged_Abundance) %>% select(-NW016_5_all_merged_Abundance)

ncol(select_path_abun)

# Filter on the prevalence and abundance of the Pathways
# Prevalence > 10%, Abundance > 0.1%/0.01%
# 3.1 转为 matrix：rows=Pathway, cols=Sample
mat_path <- select_path_abun %>%
  column_to_rownames("Pathway") %>%
  as.matrix()

# 确保都是数值
storage.mode(mat_path) <- "numeric"

# 3.2 转为 samples x pathways
mat_samp <- t(mat_path)

mat_rel <- mat_samp

# 3.4 prevalence（出现比例）与平均相对丰度
prev <- colMeans(mat_rel > 0, na.rm = TRUE)              # 每个 pathway 在多少样本中出现
mean_rel <- colMeans(mat_rel, na.rm = TRUE)              # 每个 pathway 的平均相对丰度

prev_cut <- 0.10

keep <- (prev >= prev_cut)

mat_rel_filt <- mat_rel[, keep, drop = FALSE]

# The number of left pathways
ncol(mat_rel_filt)

### Calculate the Bray-Curtis distance
dist_bc <- vegdist(mat_rel_filt, method = "bray")


### Calculate the Aitchison distance
# add a small pseudocount
pseudo <- 1e-6
mat_rel_pc <- mat_rel_filt + pseudo
# re-close to composition (optional but recommended)
mat_rel_pc <- mat_rel_pc / rowSums(mat_rel_pc)
# CLR transform
mat_clr <- clr(mat_rel_pc)
# Aitchison distance = Euclidean distance on CLR data
dist_aitchison <- dist(mat_clr, method = "euclidean")



###### Analysis based on Bray-Curtis distance
### PCoA Plot
# pcoa <- cmdscale(dist_bc, k = 2, eig = TRUE)
# pcoa_df <- as.data.frame(pcoa$points)
# colnames(pcoa_df) <- c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")
# pcoa_df$SampleID_in_table <- rownames(pcoa_df)
# 
# # 解释度（百分比）
# var_exp <- pcoa$eig / sum(pcoa$eig)
# pcoa1_lab <- paste0("PCoA1 (", round(100 * var_exp[1], 1), "%)")
# pcoa2_lab <- paste0("PCoA2 (", round(100 * var_exp[2], 1), "%)")


pcoa_res <- ape::pcoa(dist_bc)
pcoa_df <- as.data.frame(pcoa_res$vectors[, 1:2])
colnames(pcoa_df) <- c("PCoA1", "PCoA2")
pcoa_df$SampleID_in_table <- rownames(pcoa_df)

# Calculate percentage of explained variation using positive eigenvalues only
pcoa1_lab <- paste0("PCoA1 (", round(pcoa_res$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 1), "%)")
pcoa2_lab <- paste0("PCoA2 (", round(pcoa_res$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 1), "%)")

### Read in metadata
meta_file <- file.path("../../data/metadata", "selected_northwood_map.txt")
metadata <- read_tsv(meta_file, col_types = cols())

# 你的 metadata 里是 "#SampleID" 例如 "NW001-1"
metadata <- metadata %>%
  mutate(Sample_prefix = sub("-.*$", "", `#SampleID`))
head(metadata)

# 你的 pathway 表列名是 "NW001_3_all_merged_Abundance" 这种
pcoa_df <- pcoa_df %>%
  mutate(Sample_prefix = sub("_.*$", "", SampleID_in_table)) %>%
  left_join(metadata, by = "Sample_prefix")



# ### Filtering on PCoA df
pcoa_df <- pcoa_df %>%
  mutate(Update_SampleID = sub("_all_merged.*$", "", SampleID_in_table))
# head(pcoa_df)
# nrow(pcoa_df)
# filtered_pcoa_df <- pcoa_df %>% filter(`#SampleID` != "NW046-1")
# nrow(filtered_pcoa_df)
# filtered_pcoa_df %>% filter(`#SampleID` == "NW016-1")

filtered_pcoa_df <- pcoa_df



### PCoA Plot Colored with Sex (Colored based on Score)
p_pcoa <- ggplot(filtered_pcoa_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = FIScore)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  geom_text_repel(aes(label = Update_SampleID),
                  size = 3,
                  max.overlaps = 20) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma",
                        limits = c(0.3, 0.8),
                        oob = scales::squish) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "PCoA of pathway abundance (Bray-Curtis)",
    x = pcoa1_lab,
    y = pcoa2_lab
  ) +
  theme(legend.position = "right",
        plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold"))

p_pcoa

figure_file <- file.path("../../tmp_figures/PCoA_Plots", "Pathways_PCoA_Plot_labeled_rm_samp.jpg")
ggsave(filename = figure_file, plot = p_pcoa, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)





###### PERMANOVA Analyisis for All Other Factors
meta <- filtered_pcoa_df %>%
  distinct(SampleID = SampleID_in_table, Sex, FIRange, FICat, Age, TimeNW, FIScore, Location)

meta <- meta[match(labels(dist_bc), meta$SampleID), ]
stopifnot(all(meta$SampleID == labels(dist_bc)))

head(meta)
tail(meta)


set.seed(1)
# Test with FIScore
adonis_fiscore <- adonis2(dist_bc ~ FIScore, data = meta, permutations = 9999)
adonis_fiscore
# Test with Age
adonis_age <- adonis2(dist_bc ~ Age, data = meta, permutations = 9999)
adonis_age
# Test with TimeNW
adonis_timenw <- adonis2(dist_bc ~ TimeNW, data = meta, permutations = 9999)
adonis_timenw 
# Test with Location
adonis_location <- adonis2(dist_bc ~ Location, data = meta, permutations = 9999)
adonis_location
# Test with Sex
adonis_sex <- adonis2(dist_bc ~ Sex, data = meta, permutations = 9999)
adonis_sex
# Test with FICat
adonis_ficat <- adonis2(dist_bc ~ FICat, data = meta, permutations = 9999)
adonis_ficat
e

###### Multiple variates test
adonis_multi <- adonis2(
  dist_bc ~ Sex + Age + TimeNW + FIScore + Location,
  data = meta,
  permutations = 9999,
  by = "margin"
)
adonis_multi

# Output Results
res_multi <- as.data.frame(adonis_multi)
res_multi$Variable <- rownames(res_multi)
rownames(res_multi) <- NULL

res_multi_out <- res_multi %>%
  select(Variable, Df, R2, F, `Pr(>F)`)

res_multi_out

res_multi_file_name <- file.path("../../tmp_figures/PCoA_Plots", "pathway_adonis_multi_summary.tsv")
write.table(res_multi_out, res_multi_file_name,
            sep = "\t", quote = FALSE, row.names = FALSE)



# Dispersion Checking
disp_sex <- betadisper(dist_bc, meta$Sex)
anova(disp_sex)
permutest(disp_sex, permutations = 9999)

disp_ficat <- betadisper(dist_bc, meta$FICat)
anova(disp_ficat)
permutest(disp_ficat, permutations = 9999)

disp_location <- betadisper(dist_bc, meta$Location)
anova(disp_location)
permutest(disp_location, permutations = 9999)


### PCOA Plots for factor variables (FICat)
filtered_pcoa_df <- filtered_pcoa_df %>%
  mutate(FICat = factor(FICat, levels = sort(unique(FICat))))

r2_ficat <- adonis_ficat$R2[1]
p_ficat  <- adonis_ficat$`Pr(>F)`[1]

pcoa_ficat <- ggplot(filtered_pcoa_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = FICat)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "plasma", end = 0.95) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "PCoA of pathway abundance (Bray-Curtis)",
    subtitle = sprintf("PERMANOVA (FICat): R² = %.3f, p = %.4g", r2_ficat, p_ficat),
    x = pcoa1_lab,
    y = pcoa2_lab
  ) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = FICat), type = "t", level = 0.95,
               linewidth = 0.8, show.legend = FALSE) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

pcoa_ficat

figure_file <- file.path("../../tmp_figures/PCoA_Plots", "Pathways_PCoA_Plot_FICat_grouped_color.jpg")
ggsave(filename = figure_file, plot = pcoa_ficat, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)


### PCOA Plots for factor variables (Location)
r2_location <- adonis_location$R2[1]
p_location  <- adonis_location$`Pr(>F)`[1]

pcoa_location <- ggplot(filtered_pcoa_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = Location)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "plasma", end = 0.95) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "PCoA of pathway abundance (Bray-Curtis)",
    subtitle = sprintf("PERMANOVA (Location): R² = %.3f, p = %.4g", r2_location, p_location),
    x = pcoa1_lab,
    y = pcoa2_lab
  ) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Location), type = "t", level = 0.95,
               linewidth = 0.8, show.legend = FALSE) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

pcoa_location

figure_file <- file.path("../../tmp_figures/PCoA_Plots", "Pathways_PCoA_Plot_Location_grouped_color.jpg")
ggsave(filename = figure_file, plot = pcoa_location, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)


### PCOA Plots for factor variables (Sex)
r2_sex <- adonis_sex$R2[1]
p_sex  <- adonis_sex$`Pr(>F)`[1]

pcoa_sex <- ggplot(filtered_pcoa_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = Sex)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis_d(option = "plasma", end = 0.95) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "PCoA of pathway abundance (Bray-Curtis)",
    subtitle = sprintf("PERMANOVA (Sex): R² = %.3f, p = %.4g", r2_sex, p_sex),
    x = pcoa1_lab,
    y = pcoa2_lab
  ) +
  stat_ellipse(aes(group = Sex), type = "t", level = 0.95,
               linewidth = 0.8, show.legend = FALSE) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

pcoa_sex

figure_file <- file.path("../../tmp_figures/PCoA_Plots", "Pathways_PCoA_Plot_Sex_grouped_color.jpg")
ggsave(filename = figure_file, plot = pcoa_sex, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)


### PCoA Plots for continuous variables (FIScore)
r2_fiscore <- adonis_fiscore$R2[1]
p_fiscore  <- adonis_fiscore$`Pr(>F)`[1]

p_fiscore <- ggplot(filtered_pcoa_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = FIScore)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma", end = 0.95) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "PCoA of pathway abundance (Bray-Curtis)",
    subtitle = sprintf("PERMANOVA (FIScore): R² = %.3f, p = %.4g", r2_fiscore, p_fiscore),
    x = pcoa1_lab,
    y = pcoa2_lab
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

p_fiscore

figure_file <- file.path("../../tmp_figures/PCoA_Plots", "Pathways_PCoA_Plot_FIScore_color.jpg")
ggsave(filename = figure_file, plot = p_fiscore, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)



### PCoA Plots for continuous variables
r2_age <- adonis_age$R2[1]
p_age  <- adonis_age$`Pr(>F)`[1]

pcoa_age <- ggplot(filtered_pcoa_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = Age)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma", end = 0.95) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "PCoA of pathway abundance (Bray-Curtis)",
    subtitle = sprintf("PERMANOVA (Age): R² = %.3f, p = %.4g", r2_age, p_age),
    x = pcoa1_lab,
    y = pcoa2_lab
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )

pcoa_age


figure_file <- file.path("../../tmp_figures/PCoA_Plots", "Pathways_PCoA_Plot_Age_color.jpg")
ggsave(filename = figure_file, plot = pcoa_age, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)




### PCoA Plots for continuous variables (TimeNW)
r2_timenw <- adonis_timenw$R2[1]
p_timenw   <- adonis_timenw$`Pr(>F)`[1]

pcoa_timenw <- ggplot(filtered_pcoa_df, aes(x = PCoA1, y = PCoA2, color = TimeNW)) +
  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.8) +
  scale_color_viridis_c(option = "plasma", end = 0.95) +
  theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
  labs(
    title = "PCoA of pathway abundance (Bray-Curtis)",
    subtitle = sprintf("PERMANOVA (TimeNW): R² = %.3f, p = %.4g", r2_timenw, p_timenw),
    x = pcoa1_lab,
    y = pcoa2_lab
  ) +
  theme(
    legend.position = "right",
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
  )
pcoa_timenw

figure_file <- file.path("../../tmp_figures/PCoA_Plots", "Pathways_PCoA_Plot_TimeNW_color.jpg")
ggsave(filename = figure_file, plot = pcoa_timenw, width = 8, height = 6, dpi = 300)


```



### Study Pathway Relative Abundance against environmental factors
```{r}
library(dplyr)
library(readr)
library(tidyr)
library(stringr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tibble)
library(scales)
library(pheatmap)

### Readin Humann Pathway Abundance Table
infile <- file.path("../../data/Metagenomics/HUMAnN_func/Normalized_Tables", "merged_pathabundance_relab.tsv")
#outfile <- "summed_path_abundance.out"
corr_file <- file.path("../../tmp_figures", "pathway_metadata_correlations.tsv")

path_df <- read_tsv(infile) %>% rename(Pathway = `# Pathway`)


### New and more direct method to extract the total abundance of each pathway
head(path_df)
nrow(path_df)

path_merged <- path_df %>%
  filter (!grepl("\\|", Pathway))
head(path_merged)
nrow(path_merged)


# Filter pathway abundance data for pathways exists in at least 1/3 of all MG samples
sample_cols <- names(path_merged)[-1]
n_samples <- length(sample_cols)
min_present <- ceiling(n_samples / 3)
min_present

path_merged <- path_merged %>%
  filter(
    rowSums(across(all_of(sample_cols), ~ .x > 0), na.rm = TRUE) >= min_present
  )
nrow(path_merged)

### Output Results
#write.table(path_merged, outfile, row.names = FALSE, sep = "\t", quote = FALSE)


### Read in Samples Meta Table
meta_file <- "../../data/Metadata/selected_northwood_map.txt"
metadata <- read_tsv(meta_file, col_types = cols())
# 提取前缀 NW001, NW002 ...
metadata <- metadata %>%
  mutate(Sample_prefix = sub("-.*$", "", `#SampleID`))
head(metadata)
nrow(metadata)

path_long <- path_merged %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols      = -Pathway,
    names_to  = "Sample_col",
    values_to = "Abundance"
  ) %>%
  # 提取 NW001, NW002 这种前缀
  mutate(Sample_prefix = sub("_.*$", "", Sample_col))

head(path_long)
nrow(path_long)
#table(path_long$Pathway)
#table(path_long$Sample_col)


### Remove duplicate NW016 samples in the MG pathway abundance table
selected_path_long <- path_long %>% filter(Sample_col != "NW016_2_2_all_merged_Abundance") %>% filter(Sample_col != "NW016_5_all_merged_Abundance")
nrow(selected_path_long)

### Remove the NW046 sample which does not have Metadata
selected_path_long <- selected_path_long %>% filter(Sample_col != "NW046_5_all_merged_Abundance")
nrow(selected_path_long)



### Add metadata to the MG pathway abundance table
path_meta <- selected_path_long %>% left_join(metadata, by = "Sample_prefix")

# Modification to variables
path_meta2 <- path_meta %>%
  mutate(
    Sex_num = case_when(
      Sex == "Male"   ~ 1,
      Sex == "Female" ~ 0,
      TRUE            ~ NA_real_
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    Location_num = case_when(
      Location == "Center"   ~ 1,
      Location == "Manor" ~ 0,
      TRUE            ~ NA_real_
    )
  )  

vars <- c("Age", "TimeNW", "FIScore", "Sex_num", "FICat", "Location_num")
binary_vars <- c("Sex", "Location", "FICat")



###### Calculate pathway differences across binary factors
binary_list <- lapply(binary_vars, function(v) {
  path_meta %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[v]]), !is.na(Abundance)) %>%
    group_by(Pathway) %>%
    summarise(
      n = n(),
      n_group = n_distinct(.data[[v]]),
      stat = if (
        n_group[1] == 2
      ) {
        suppressWarnings(wilcox.test(Abundance ~ .data[[v]])$statistic)
      } else {
        NA_real_
      },
      p = if (
        n_group[1] == 2
      ) {
        suppressWarnings(wilcox.test(Abundance ~ .data[[v]])$p.value)
      } else {
        NA_real_
      },
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(variable = v, test = "wilcox") %>%
    select(Pathway, variable, test, n, n_group, stat, p)
})

test_table_binary <- bind_rows(binary_list)
test_table_binary <- test_table_binary %>%
  group_by(variable) %>%
  mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(p, method = "fdr")) %>%
  ungroup()


head(test_table_binary)

table(test_table_binary$variable)
filtered_test_table_binary <- test_table_binary %>% filter(p_adj < 0.05)
nrow(filtered_test_table_binary)
# filtered_test_table_binary <- test_table_binary %>% filter(p < 0.01)
# nrow(filtered_test_table_binary)

table_file <- file.path("../../tmp_figures", "pathway_metadata_binary_test.tsv")
write_tsv(test_table_binary, table_file)



###### Heatmap plot for pathways with significant changes across locations
loc_sig_pathways <- filtered_test_table_binary %>%
  filter(variable == "Location") %>%
  pull(Pathway) %>%
  unique()

# Extract pathway abundance matrix
heatmap_df <- selected_path_long %>%
  filter(Pathway %in% loc_sig_pathways)

heatmap_df <- heatmap_df %>%
  select(Pathway, Sample_col, Abundance) %>%
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Sample_col,
    values_from = Abundance
  )

heatmap_mat <- heatmap_df %>%
  column_to_rownames("Pathway") %>%
  as.matrix()

storage.mode(heatmap_mat) <- "numeric"

# Prepare sample annotation using metadata
sample_anno <- data.frame(
  SampleID_in_table = colnames(heatmap_mat),
  Sample_prefix = sub("_.*$", "", colnames(heatmap_mat))
) %>%
  left_join(metadata %>% select(Sample_prefix, Location), by = "Sample_prefix")

annotation_col <- sample_anno %>%
  select(Location)
rownames(annotation_col) <- sample_anno$SampleID_in_table
annotation_col <- annotation_col[colnames(heatmap_mat), , drop = FALSE]

# Simplify column names in the heatmap matrix
new_colnames <- sub("_all_merged_Abundance.*$", "", colnames(heatmap_mat))
colnames(heatmap_mat) <- new_colnames
rownames(annotation_col) <- new_colnames

# Optional annotation colors
ann_colors <- list(
  Location = c(
    Center = "#1f78b4",
    Manor = "#33a02c"
  )
)


### Draw and save heatmap
figure_file <- file.path("../../tmp_figures/", "Location_Pathways_heatmap.jpg")
jpeg(filename = figure_file, width = 8, height = 6, units = "in", res = 300)

pheatmap(
  heatmap_mat,
  scale = "row",
  annotation_col = annotation_col,
  annotation_colors = ann_colors,
  cluster_rows = TRUE,
  cluster_cols = TRUE,
  show_rownames = TRUE,
  show_colnames = TRUE,
  fontsize_row = 8,
  fontsize_col = 7,
  main = "Significant pathways associated with Location\n(Row Z-score)"
)
#print(loc_path_heatmap)

dev.off()



###### Calculate correlations between pathway abundance and individual factors
corr_list <- lapply(vars, function(v) {
  # 把 Sex_num, Location_num 这个内部名字映射回更好看的标签
  var_label <- dplyr::case_when(
    v == "Sex_num"      ~ "Sex",
    v == "Location_num" ~ "Location",
    TRUE ~ v
  )
  
  path_meta2 %>%
    group_by(Pathway) %>%
    summarise(
      n = sum(!is.na(Abundance) & !is.na(.data[[v]])),
      cor = ifelse(
        n >= 3,
        suppressWarnings(cor(Abundance, .data[[v]],
                             method = "spearman",
                             use = "complete.obs")),
        NA_real_
      ),
      p = ifelse(
        n >= 3,
        suppressWarnings(cor.test(Abundance, .data[[v]],
                                  method = "spearman",
                                  exact = FALSE)$p.value),
        NA_real_
      ),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    mutate(variable = var_label) %>%
    select(Pathway, variable, n, cor, p)
})

corr_table <- bind_rows(corr_list)
head(corr_table)
table(corr_table$variable)

### FDR adjust
corr_table <- corr_table %>%
  group_by(variable) %>%
  mutate(p_adj = p.adjust(p, method = "fdr")) %>%
  ungroup()

# ### Output correlation results
# write_tsv(corr_table, corr_file)


### Quick Sta
#corr_table <- read.table(corr_file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t")
#p-adj filter
nrow(corr_table %>% filter(p_adj < 0.05))
p_adj_filter_corr_table <- corr_table %>% filter(p_adj < 0.05)
table(p_adj_filter_corr_table$variable)

# #p value filter
# nrow(corr_table %>% filter(p < 0.01))
# p_filter_corr_table <- corr_table %>% filter(p < 0.01)
# table(p_filter_corr_table$variable)







###### Scatter Plots for pathways
### Plots for age-associated pathways
# 从 corr_table 里筛选 Age 显著的 pathway 名
paths_of_interest <- corr_table %>%
  filter(p_adj < 0.05, variable == "Age") %>%
  pull(Pathway) %>%
  unique()

paths_of_interest

plot_df <- path_meta2 %>%
  filter(Pathway %in% paths_of_interest)

# 为了避免 y 轴太极端，可以视情况加上 log10 转换
make_scatter <- function(df, pw_name) {
  safe_name <- pw_name %>%
    str_replace(":.*$", "") 

  ggplot(df, aes(x = Age, y = Abundance)) +  
    geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.5) +
    geom_smooth(method = "lm", se = TRUE, color = "red") +
    scale_y_continuous(labels = label_scientific()) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      panel.grid = element_blank(),
      plot.title = element_text(face = "bold", hjust = 0.5),
      axis.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
      axis.text = element_text(color = "black")
    ) +
    labs(
      title = safe_name,
      x = "Age",
      y = "Pathway relative abundance"
    )
}



for (pw in paths_of_interest) {
  
  df_pw <- plot_df %>% filter(Pathway == pw)

  # 生成安全的文件名：去掉奇怪符号，太长就截断
  safe_name <- pw %>%
    str_replace(":.*$", "") 

  p <- make_scatter(df_pw, pw)
  
  figure_file <- file.path("../../tmp_figures", paste0("Age_vs_", safe_name, ".jpg"))

  ggsave(
    filename = figure_file,
    plot     = p,
    width    = 5,
    height   = 4,
    dpi      = 300
  )
}

```



### Analysis with MaAsLin3
```{r}
setwd("C:/Users/dlszz/OneDrive - Dalhousie University/Nursing_House_Project/Git_Project/northwood-microbiome/code/MG_analysis")

library(maaslin3)

# ### Read in HUMANn pathway abundance table
# #data_table_name <- system.file("extdata", "HMP2_taxonomy.tsv", package = "maaslin3")
# #data_table_name <- file.path("../../data/Metagenomics/HUMAnN_func", "nursing_house_humman_cov30_path_abundance.out")
# data_table_name <- file.path("../../data/Metagenomics/HUMAnN_func/Normalized_Tables", "merged_pathabundance_relab.tsv")
# 
# org_data_table <- read.csv(data_table_name, sep = '\t', row.names = 1)
# nrow(org_data_table)
# clean_data_table <- org_data_table[!grepl("\\|", rownames(org_data_table)), ]
# nrow(clean_data_table)
# # clean_data_table <- clean_data_table[!rownames(clean_data_table) %in% c("UNMAPPED", "UNINTEGRATED"), ]
# nrow(clean_data_table)
# data_table <- t(clean_data_table)
# 
# # Remove Duplicate Samples and Sample without metadata
# data_table <- data_table[!rownames(data_table) %in% c("NW016_2_2_all_merged_Abundance", "NW016_5_all_merged_Abundance", "NW046_5_all_merged_Abundance"), ]
# data_table[1:5, 1:5]
# nrow(data_table)



# ### Read in HUMANn gene family abundance table
# data_table_name <- file.path("../../data/Metagenomics/HUMAnN_func", "nursing_house_humman_cov30_gene_families_anno.out")
# org_data_table <- read.csv(data_table_name, sep = '\t', row.names = 1, check.names = FALSE)
# nrow(org_data_table)
# clean_data_table <- org_data_table[!grepl("\\|", rownames(org_data_table)), ]
# nrow(clean_data_table)
# data_table <- t(clean_data_table)
# 
# # Remove Duplicate Samples and Sample without metadata
# data_table <- data_table[!rownames(data_table) %in% c("NW016_2_2_all_merged_Abundance-RPKs", "NW016_5_all_merged_Abundance-RPKs", "NW046_5_all_merged_Abundance-RPKs"), ]
# data_table[1:5, 1:5]
# nrow(data_table)
# ncol(data_table)
# # Remove column(gene family) with non-zero values in <10% rows(samples)
# nonzero_counts <- colSums(data_table != 0)
# threshold <- ceiling(0.8 * nrow(data_table))
# data_table<- data_table[, nonzero_counts >= threshold]
# ncol(data_table)



### Read in MetaPhlAn taxa abundance table
data_table_name <- file.path("../../data/Metagenomics/MetaPhlAn_taxa", "merged_abundance_table.txt")
org_data_table <- read.csv(data_table_name, sep = '\t', row.names = 1, comment.char = "#")
nrow(org_data_table)

# Select rows with species level annotation
clean_data_table <- org_data_table[
  grepl("\\|s__", rownames(org_data_table)) &
  !grepl("\\|t__", rownames(org_data_table)),
]

# # Select rows with genus level annotation
# clean_data_table <- org_data_table[
#   grepl("\\|g__", rownames(org_data_table)) &
#   !grepl("\\|s__", rownames(org_data_table)),
# ]

# # Select rows with family level annotation
# clean_data_table <- org_data_table[
#   grepl("\\|f__", rownames(org_data_table)) &
#   !grepl("\\|g__", rownames(org_data_table)),
# ]

# # Select rows with phylum level annotation
# clean_data_table <- org_data_table[
#   grepl("\\|p__", rownames(org_data_table)) &
#   !grepl("\\|c__", rownames(org_data_table)),
# ]

nrow(clean_data_table)
data_table <- t(clean_data_table)

# Remove Duplicate Samples and Sample without metadata
data_table <- data_table[!rownames(data_table) %in% c("NW016_2_2_all", "NW016_5_all", "NW046_5_all"), ]
colnames(data_table) <- sub(".*\\|s__", "", colnames(data_table))
# colnames(data_table) <- sub(".*\\|g__", "", colnames(data_table))
# colnames(data_table) <- sub(".*\\|f__", "", colnames(data_table))
# colnames(data_table) <- sub(".*\\|p__", "", colnames(data_table))


data_table[1:5, 1:5]
nrow(data_table)
ncol(data_table)


### Read in metadata table
# metadata_name <- system.file("extdata", "HMP2_metadata.tsv", package = "maaslin3")
metadata_name <- file.path("../../data/Metadata", "selected_northwood_map.txt")
org_metadata <- read.csv(metadata_name, sep = '\t', row.names = 1)
# Remove sample without metadata
metadata <- org_metadata[!rownames(org_metadata) %in% c("NW046-1"), ]
head(metadata)
nrow(metadata)

# Matching ID names
# 1. 提取核心 ID
data_id <- sub("_.*", "", rownames(data_table))
meta_id <- sub("-.*", "", rownames(metadata))
# 2. 匹配
match_idx <- match(meta_id, data_id)
# 3. 去掉无法匹配的
keep <- !is.na(match_idx)
metadata_matched <- metadata[keep, ]
match_idx <- match_idx[keep]
# 4. 替换 rownames
rownames(metadata_matched) <- rownames(data_table)[match_idx]
metadata <- metadata_matched
head(metadata)



### Factor the categorical variables in metadata
metadata$Sex <- 
  factor(metadata$Sex, levels = c('Male', 'Female'))

metadata$Location <- 
  factor(metadata$Location, levels = c('Manor', 'Center'))

metadata$FICat <- 
  factor(metadata$FICat, levels = c('3', '4'))

# Check dataframes
data_table[1:5, 1:5]
metadata[1:5, ]


### Running MaAsLin3
set.seed(1)
fit_out <- maaslin3(input_data = data_table,
                    input_metadata = metadata,
                    output = 'MaAsLin3_test_output',
                    formula = '~ Sex + Age +
                        TimeNW + FIScore + Location',
                    normalization = 'TSS',
                    transform = 'LOG',
                    augment = TRUE,
                    standardize = TRUE,
                    max_significance = 0.1,
                    median_comparison_abundance = TRUE,
                    median_comparison_prevalence = FALSE,
                    max_pngs = 250,
                    cores = 4)


```



Note that the `echo = FALSE` parameter was added to the code chunk to prevent printing of the R code that generated the plot.
